policy_statement: By 2023, inventory natural gas-fueled equipment in municipal buildings (BO.2-4).
mechanism_description: Performs an inventory of natural gas-fueled equipment in municipal buildings to support planning for future emissions-reduction actions.
primary_causal_pathway: Information/asset inventory → improved decarbonization planning & targeting → future fuel-switching/efficiency actions → ↓ fossil fuel combustion → ↓ GHG emissions
causal_mechanism_detail: (1) Changes the information base and management of municipal building assets by identifying and characterizing natural gas-fueled end-use equipment (e.g., boilers, water heaters, cooking equipment). (2) The mediating process is improved planning, prioritization, and scheduling of replacements/retrofits (e.g., at end-of-life) and identification of infrastructure constraints, which increases feasibility and uptake of electrification and/or efficiency measures. (3) The climate outcome is reduced direct (Scope 1) combustion of fossil gas in buildings over time, leading to lower CO₂ (and some methane leakage-related) emissions.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is enabling future electrification/retrofits that reduce on-site fossil gas combustion and associated GHG emissions in buildings.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: the inventory supports subsequent actions whose principal effect is reduced fossil fuel combustion emissions in buildings (mitigation).  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: it is not Resource Efficiency by itself because no direct reduction in energy/material inputs is achieved at the inventory stage; not Adaptation because it does not primarily reduce hazard exposure/vulnerability; not Nature-Based Solutions because it does not act through ecosystems.  
3) Edge cases applied: while inventories can have administrative co-benefits, the mechanism is explicitly tied to “future emissions-reduction actions,” so the dominant intended climate-relevant pathway is mitigation-enabling.  
4) Confirm applies to ALL instances: any policy that inventories gas-fueled equipment for decarbonization planning is inherently aimed at enabling emissions reductions from fossil fuel use; the primary category remains Mitigation even if downstream measures vary.  
5) Note on secondary subtypes: any finer subtype (e.g., “planning/information,” “municipal operations,” “building electrification enabling”) must be assigned at the individual policy level based on additional evidence of intended downstream actions and scope.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If an inventory is performed solely for safety, maintenance, or cost control without a decarbonization objective, the dominant pathway could shift away from mitigation; here the mechanism explicitly links the inventory to emissions-reduction planning, supporting Mitigation.
instance_edge_case_notes: Classified as information-disclosure (data collection/inventory). Could also be construed as a planning-strategy precursor, but the explicit instrument in the text is the inventory itself rather than a plan or capital program.
